content: Treatment depends on stage. Localized (stage I-II): wide local excision with margins based on thickness (in situ 0.5-1cm, ≤1mm 1cm, 1-2mm 1-2cm, >2mm 2cm); sentinel lymph node biopsy for tumors >0.8mm or <0.8mm with ulceration/high mitotic rate. Stage III (node-positive): completion lymph node dissection no longer routine after positive SLN (MSLT-II); adjuvant therapy - anti-PD-1 (pembrolizumab/nivolumab) for resected stage III-IV (KEYNOTE-054, CheckMate 238); BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib + trametinib, encorafenib + binimetinib) for BRAF-mutated stage III (COMBI-AD). Metastatic (stage IV): immunotherapy first-line - anti-PD-1 monotherapy or combination ipilimumab + nivolumab (CheckMate 067, superior PFS/OS but more toxicity); BRAF/MEK combination for BRAF-mutated (dabrafenib + trametinib, encorafenib + binimetinib, vemurafenib + cobimetinib); T-VEC (oncolytic virus) for injectable lesions. Second-line: ipilimumab if not previously given; clinical trials. Brain metastases: stereotactic radiosurgery; immunotherapy has CNS activity.
